method: Action-angle closure test for the 8H = 2,682,536 yr period. For each candidate lag τ, computed normalized closure distance D(τ) = ⟨‖state(t+τ) − state(t)‖⟩ / σ_state. Compared D(8H) to D(τ) at random non-8H-related lags (avoiding ±5% of any n×8H). Tested both (h, k) = (e sin ϖ, e cos ϖ) and obliquity. Also tested rational-frequency-ratios: do top spectral peak frequencies satisfy f × 8H ≈ integer?
verdict: ✓ 8H IS A CLOSED-ORBIT PERIOD FOR OBLIQUITY but NOT for the full eccentricity vector. Obliquity closes at 8H (only 2.5% of random lags beat it), and 8/10 obliquity peaks land within 5% of integer divisors of 1/(8H) — most within 0.2%. Eccentricity vector (h, k) shows no preferred closure at 8H, but the eccentricity SCALAR spectrum does have 6/10 top peaks at integer divisors. The asymmetry matches Test C-Invariant: obliquity is 100% on lattice, eccentricity is 74% (with 26% Mercury-chaos perturbation off-lattice). The perturbation channel breaks exact closure for (h,k) but obliquity's spectrum is genuinely quantized on integer divisors of 8H.
